name: CRDCoverageInformation
title: Coverage Information
description: Captures assertions from a payer about the coverage rules for a service - in particular, whether it is covered and/or requires prior authorization.

element.definition: Indicates coverage information.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both
Questionnaire is only allowed when doc-needed exists
If covered is set to 'not-covered', then 'pa-needed' must not exist.
'info-needed' SHALL exist if and only if at least one of 'covered', 'pa-needed', or 'doc-needed' is set to 'conditional'.
constraint.expression: extension.where((url = 'covered' or url = 'pa-needed' or url = 'doc-needed') and value = 'conditional').count() >= 1 implies extension.where(url = 'info-needed').exists()

constraint.human: If 'pa-needed' is 'satisfied', 'noauth', or 'not-covered', then 'Doc-purpose' cannot be 'withpa'.
constraint.expression: extension.where(url = 'pa-needed' and (value = 'satisfied' or value = 'noauth' or value = 'not-covered')) and extension.where(url = 'doc-purpose').exists() implies extension.where(url = 'doc-purpose').all(value != 'withpa')

constraint.human: 'satisfied-pa-id' must exist if and only if 'pa-needed' is set to 'satisfied'.
If 'info-needed' is OTH, then reason must be specified
If reason.coding is present and is not from the extensible value set, then reason.text must be present
If doc-purpose is present with a value other than 'conditional', then reason must be present
If any of 'covered', 'pa-needed', or 'doc-needed' is set to 'indeterminate', then reason must be present to explain the issue that is preventing the payer from making a coverage determination.
constraint.expression: extension.where((url = 'covered' or url = 'pa-needed' or url = 'doc-needed') and value = 'indeterminate').count() >= 1 implies extension.where(url = 'reason').exists()

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: An Extension

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: Reference to Coverage that assertion applies to.
element.comment: This will always be a link to a Coverage resource on the CRD client's system - typically accessed via prefetch or direct query using the CDS-Hooks token.  If no coverage instance is available, the coverage-information extension cannot be returned.

element.short: identifies the meaning of the extension
element.definition: Source of the definition for the extension code - a logical name or a URL.
element.comment: The definition may point directly to a computable or human-readable definition of the extensibility codes, or it may be a logical URI as declared in some other specification. The definition SHALL be a URI for the Structure Definition defining the extension.

element.definition: Value of extension - must be one of a constrained set of the data types (see [Extensibility](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html) for a list).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Indicates whether the ordered/requested service is covered under patient's plan.  This includes checking whether the proposed service is a benefit under the patient's plan.  It **MAY** also involve checking whether the patient has reached their limits under the current plan period but is not required to.
element.comment: Details on why coverage does not exist would be conveyed in the 'reason' element.

element.short: performer | location | timeframe | contract-window | detail-code | OTH
element.definition: Indicates whether information about the performer, location, and/or performance date is needed to determine coverage information
element.comment:
See additional guidance in the [notes](StructureDefinition-ext-coverage-information.html#doc-needed-vs-info-needed)
If this element is set to OTH, then there must be at least one detail element the explains the nature of the additional information needed to determine coverage, prior authorization, and/or documentation requirements.  There **SHOULD** also be a doc-needed element indicating that DTR can be used to gather additional information.

element.definition: Indicates the 'reason' for the coverage assertion
element.comment: This can be used whenever the reason may not be obvious to the practitioner.  E.g. prior authorization is waived because the provider is gold-carded; patient is no longer a minor and has not been registered as an adult dependent; patient has reached their limit for this type of service this year; etc.  Additional standard reason codes may be introduced in the future.  If no standard code applies, use text.

element.definition: If present, indicates that the determination represented here is dependent on the content, determination, and possibly execution of the referenced order(s)
element.requirements: For example, the authorization decision on a request for post-surgical physiotherapy might be dependent on the order for the surgery itself.  If coverage for the surgery is not approved or the order for the surgery is cancelled, that might impact the decision on covering the physiotherapy.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Indicates coverage information.

constraint.human: Questionnaire is only allowed when doc-needed exists
If covered is set to 'not-covered', then 'pa-needed' must not exist.
'info-needed' SHALL exist if and only if at least one of 'covered', 'pa-needed', or 'doc-needed' is set to 'conditional'.
constraint.expression: extension.where((url = 'covered' or url = 'pa-needed' or url = 'doc-needed') and value = 'conditional').count() >= 1 implies extension.where(url = 'info-needed').exists()

constraint.human: If 'pa-needed' is 'satisfied', 'noauth', or 'not-covered', then 'Doc-purpose' cannot be 'withpa'.
constraint.expression: extension.where(url = 'pa-needed' and (value = 'satisfied' or value = 'noauth' or value = 'not-covered')) and extension.where(url = 'doc-purpose').exists() implies extension.where(url = 'doc-purpose').all(value != 'withpa')

constraint.human: 'satisfied-pa-id' must exist if and only if 'pa-needed' is set to 'satisfied'.
If 'info-needed' is OTH, then reason must be specified
If reason.coding is present and is not from the extensible value set, then reason.text must be present
If doc-purpose is present with a value other than 'conditional', then reason must be present
If any of 'covered', 'pa-needed', or 'doc-needed' is set to 'indeterminate', then reason must be present to explain the issue that is preventing the payer from making a coverage determination.
constraint.expression: extension.where((url = 'covered' or url = 'pa-needed' or url = 'doc-needed') and value = 'indeterminate').count() >= 1 implies extension.where(url = 'reason').exists()

element.definition: Reference to Coverage that assertion applies to.
element.comment: This will always be a link to a Coverage resource on the CRD client's system - typically accessed via prefetch or direct query using the CDS-Hooks token.  If no coverage instance is available, the coverage-information extension cannot be returned.

element.definition: Indicates whether the ordered/requested service is covered under patient's plan.  This includes checking whether the proposed service is a benefit under the patient's plan.  It **MAY** also involve checking whether the patient has reached their limits under the current plan period but is not required to.
element.comment: Details on why coverage does not exist would be conveyed in the 'reason' element.

element.short: no-auth | auth-needed | satisfied | performpa | conditional | indeterminate
element.definition: Indicates whether prior auth will be needed for coverage to be provided
Indicates whether additional documentation needs to be captured (purpose in next element)
element.comment: See additional guidance in the [notes](StructureDefinition-ext-coverage-information.html#doc-needed-vs-info-needed)

element.definition: Indicates the reason(s) for the additional documentation that needs to be captured
element.requirements: Knowing what the information will be used for may influence who will fill out the form(s) and how quickly the organization will get around to filling them out

element.short: performer | location | timeframe | contract-window | detail-code | OTH
element.definition: Indicates whether information about the performer, location, and/or performance date is needed to determine coverage information
element.comment:
See additional guidance in the [notes](StructureDefinition-ext-coverage-information.html#doc-needed-vs-info-needed)
If this element is set to OTH, then there must be at least one detail element the explains the nature of the additional information needed to determine coverage, prior authorization, and/or documentation requirements.  There **SHOULD** also be a doc-needed element indicating that DTR can be used to gather additional information.

element.definition: Billing code(s) that must be used in the eventual claim for the coverage assertion to hold
element.comment: Codes may include modifier codes, leveraging [CPT's post-coordination syntax](https://terminology.hl7.org/CPT.html)

element.definition: Indicates the 'reason' for the coverage assertion
element.comment: This can be used whenever the reason may not be obvious to the practitioner.  E.g. prior authorization is waived because the provider is gold-carded; patient is no longer a minor and has not been registered as an adult dependent; patient has reached their limit for this type of service this year; etc.  Additional standard reason codes may be introduced in the future.  If no standard code applies, use text.

element.definition: Indicates the 'detail' for the coverage assertion
element.comment: Additional information that qualifies, describes, or provides additional guidance about coverage or documentation requirements.

element.definition: Indicates the nature of the detail, which in turn provides guidance for when it should be displayed.
element.comment: CRD clients **SHOULD** make both limitation and decisional details available to clinical users.

element.definition: The type of detail or qualification expressed.
element.comment: The binding to the old temporary code system is retained for now for backward compatibility

element.definition: The detail or qualification that applies to this coverage assertion.
Additional text that qualifies/expands on the computable detail.  E.g. 'Provided coverage is renewed' or 'Does not account for deductible'
If present, indicates that the determination represented here is dependent on the content, determination, and possibly execution of the referenced order(s)
element.requirements: For example, the authorization decision on a request for post-surgical physiotherapy might be dependent on the order for the surgery itself.  If coverage for the surgery is not approved or the order for the surgery is cancelled, that might impact the decision on covering the physiotherapy.

element.definition: A form to be filled out to gather more information.
element.comment: If using DTR, this will be used by DTR to determine the Questionnaire packages to be returned.  If empty, DTR will determine the questionnaire packages itself.  If not using DTR, providing a Questionnaire url is the only way to indicate what form to complete.

element.definition: Date on which assertion was made.
Trace identifier to allow tracking the guidance in source system.  This identifier can also be used to re-establish cached context information when subsequently launching DTR.
An identifier indicating that prior authorization requirements have been met
element.comment: When operating under the CMS enforcement discretion, this element is a prior authorization number and can be submitted in the corresponding X12 element when submitting an eventual claim

element.definition: Name, phone number, fax number, email address, website, or other contact information that can be used to ask questions/escalate issues related to a coverage assertion.
element.comment: This **SHOULD** only be populated if the contact information is context-specific rather than a generic contact for the payer as a whole.

element.definition: Date after which the coverage assertion would no longer be valid.
element.comment: In this case, mustSupport means that if the payer knows of an expiry date, they must share it.  However, if the payer never has expiry dates for their assertions, it is fine to omit.
